commit.message:
Re-enable symbol stripping for Apple platforms (#124266)

## Description

Re-enables symbol stripping for Apple platforms by removing the
`StripSymbols=false` workarounds from `ILCompiler_publish.csproj`,
`crossgen2_publish.csproj`, `ilasm.csproj`, and
`XUnitLogChecker.csproj`. Apple's pre-built Swift runtime static
libraries contain DWARF debug info with `-gmodules` references to `.pcm`
module cache files that only existed on Apple's build machines, causing
`dsymutil` to output `error:` messages even though it exits with code 0.

To handle this, `IgnoreStandardErrorWarningFormat="true"` is added to
the dsymutil `Exec` tasks in `Microsoft.NETCore.Native.targets`,
`Microsoft.NET.CrossGen.targets`, and `native-library.targets`,
preventing MSBuild from incorrectly counting these output lines as build
errors while still catching real failures via non-zero exit codes.

Additionally, Apple platform detection in `eng/toolAot.targets` is fixed
by using `PortableOS` instead of `_IsApplePlatform` (which is
unavailable at that evaluation point) and broadening the condition to
`UseNativeAotForComponents` to cover components like XUnitLogChecker
that set `PublishAot` directly.

Fixes https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/123687

---------

commit.message:
Increase default epochs to 2000 and add pixel-stable early stopping

Training could stall on corridor-shaped data: once most points were
correctly classified, sigmoid saturation made gradients near-zero,
and 500 epochs wasn't enough for the boundary to finish rotating.

Changes:
- model.py: default epochs 500 -> 2000, add patience=200 kwarg that
  stops training when the quantized pixel bytes are unchanged for 200
  consecutive epochs (keyword-only to preserve positional compat)
- cli.py: match new default epochs
- docs/app.js, docs/index.html: mirror both changes in the browser demo
- tests: add test_early_stopping, update test_training_history for new API

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[crossgen2] Add --strip-il-bodies option for composite R2R (#125647)

## Description

Add a `--strip-il-bodies` crossgen2 option that replaces IL method
bodies of successfully R2R-compiled methods with minimal stubs (`ret`)
in composite output assemblies. This reduces assembly size for scenarios
where IL interpreter fallback is not needed, such as full R2R on
iOS/MacCatalyst.

Fixes https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/124860

commit.message:
Fix ReboxLocal missing from bytecode merge/adjust and eval for-loop local visibility

Three locations scanned eval/module bytecode to skip operands but did not
account for ReboxLocal (opcode 109, 1-byte operand):

1. merge_eval_chunk in vm.rs — bytecode copier that adjusts constant
   indices and jump targets when merging eval code into the host chunk.
2. Non-configurable property check scanner in vm.rs — pre-scan of eval
   bytecode to detect illegal redefinitions.
3. adjust_merged_bytecode in opcode.rs — module bytecode IP/constant
   offset adjuster.

Missing ReboxLocal caused its 1-byte operand to be interpreted as the
next opcode, shifting all subsequent bytes and corrupting the entire
code stream. This manifested as panics (index out of bounds, unhandled
opcode 109) when eval or module code contained for-let loops.

Also extended top_level_locals_at_ip snapshots to cover all scope depths
at function_depth==0 (not just scope_depth==0), and added snapshot
recording at for-loop init entry/exit. This allows eval inside a
top-level for(let ...) body to see the loop variable.

Fixes: tagged-template/cache-eval-inner-function (cluster 15)
Fixes: module-code/namespace/define-own-property (clusters 10, 18)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
build: add clean and distclean targets

- CMake's built-in 'clean' target removes build artifacts
- New 'distclean' target (cmake/distclean.cmake) removes the build
  directory and Python-generated files from the source tree:
  - __pycache__, *.egg-info, .eggs, dist directories
  - Loose zedit-*.tar.gz / zedit-*.deb archives in the source root
- Build dir is removed via a spawned cmake -E rm subprocess to avoid
  the Linux restriction that prevents deleting a directory that is
  the current working directory of a running process
- Paths passed without shell quoting to avoid CMake VERBATIM escaping

Usage:
  cmake --build <build-dir> --target clean      # remove build artefacts
  cmake --build <build-dir> --target distclean  # nuke build dir + caches

commit.message:
feat: add Ubuntu 24.04 system config with rich file-type mappings

- Add _SYSTEM_CONFIG_TOML with mappings for common file types:
  • PDF/PS/EPS     → evince
  • .doc/.docx/.odt/.rtf → libreoffice --writer
  • .xls/.xlsx/.ods/.csv → libreoffice --calc
  • .ppt/.pptx/.odp      → libreoffice --impress
  • Images (png/jpg/gif/webp/bmp/tiff/heic) → eog
  • .svg                  → inkscape
  • Audio (mp3/ogg/flac/wav/m4a/aac) → rhythmbox
  • Video (mp4/mkv/avi/mov/webm/wmv) → totem
  • Archives (zip/tar/gz/7z/rar/zst) → file-roller
  • E-books (epub/mobi)   → foliate
  • All text/code         → $EDITOR (unchanged)

- Add write_system_config() with permission check and sudo hint
- --init-config now writes only user config (~/.config/zedit/)
- Add -f/--force flag: when combined with --init-config, also writes
  system config to /opt/etc/zedit/config.toml (sudo required if not root)
- Update config/default.toml (CMake-installed system config) to match
